response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial domain expertise from GISCollective and strong software engineering practices (TDD, testing), they lack the primary technical requirement: modern C++ development. Most recent C++ experience is from 2014-2015; the past decade has been web/full-stack work in TypeScript and Node.js. Rust is not mentioned. The role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust, and jumping from web development to systems-level routing pipelines represents too large a skill gap to bridge during onboarding.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain knowledge from founding and maintaining GISCollective, a production platform used by real organizations
Strong software engineering fundamentals with emphasis on testing, TDD, and quality assurance across all roles
Demonstrated ability to learn multiple tech stacks and architectural patterns (microservices, distributed systems, infrastructure)
concerns: C++ expertise gap: most recent commercial C++ work ended 11 years ago (2014-2015); no modern C++ demonstrated
No Rust experience at all, eliminating the acceptable alternative language required by the role
Recent career trajectory (5+ years) focused on web/full-stack development, not systems programming or data pipeline infrastructure
